fields.summary: Support multi-label merge into one node label

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description:
Support multi-label merge into one node label
1. we want to support multo-label like SSD,GPU,FPGA label merged into single machine, joined by &. like SSD,GPU,FPGA-> SSD&GPU&FPGA
2. we add wildcard match to extend the job request. we define the wildcard like *GPU*, it will math all the node labels with GPU as part of multi-label merged label. For example, *GPU* will match  SSD&GPU&FPGA, GPU&FPGA. We define  SSD&GPU&FPGA={SSD,GPU,FPGA}, and GPU is one of {SSD,GPU,FPGA}, so the job can run on  SSD&GPU&FPGA node.

fields.summary: Matcher for complex node label expresions

status.description: A patch for this issue has been uploaded to JIRA by a contributor.
status.name: Patch Available

comments.body:
Hi [~wjlei],
This seem to look like a temporary solution for the Constrain Label feature (YARN-3409).
But as i am working on a temporary patch and design for the same, i think this feature can be avoided. Will update this jira once i post temp patch and design doc in YARN-3409.

This message was automatically generated.

yes, as current node label will divide the current cluster into several sub-clusters. If Constrain Label feature ready, it will help on this. So it is a  temporary solution to solve the current situation. Welcome to update this.

Hi Jialei, this one is different than Constrain Label. Constrain Label is implemented in parallel with the node label expression, this is a enhancement to the node label expression. Normally a node label expression is a resource pool, if user want's to use more resource pools for one application, there is no way at current. Introducing node label wildcard matching is a way to solve this situation. In order to make matching more reasonable, wildcard pattern can only match the full substrings separated by "&".

This message was automatically generated.
Agree with Bin, [~Naganarasimha] Can you consider Bin's suggestion?

[~wjlei] & [~binw205],
Agree this is the need and it was suggested earlier in other jira too. But IMO its not required to mention a partition expression for a resource request at all. if the partition is available under a queue, it should be used for allocation. So its more like node constraint/attribute to be used to identify the node for a particular type. And the accessibility of a pool/partition to a queue should govern which apps can make use of a particular type.  I wanted to bring in this functionality once the Node Attributes are in. hence had not made any progress on this. 
And i apologize for the delay in YARN-3409, will kick start that work shortly.

This message was automatically generated.

yes, as current node label will divide the current cluster into several sub-clusters. If Constrain Label feature ready, it will help on this. So it is a  temporary solution to solve the current situation. Welcome to update this.

Hi Jialei, this one is different than Constrain Label. Constrain Label is implemented in parallel with the node label expression, this is a enhancement to the node label expression. Normally a node label expression is a resource pool, if user want's to use more resource pools for one application, there is no way at current. Introducing node label wildcard matching is a way to solve this situation. In order to make matching more reasonable, wildcard pattern can only match the full substrings separated by "&".

Agree with Bin, [~Naganarasimha] Can you consider Bin's suggestion?

[~wjlei] & [~binw205],
Agree this is the need and it was suggested earlier in other jira too. But IMO its not required to mention a partition expression for a resource request at all. if the partition is available under a queue, it should be used for allocation. So its more like node constraint/attribute to be used to identify the node for a particular type. And the accessibility of a pool/partition to a queue should govern which apps can make use of a particular type.  I wanted to bring in this functionality once the Node Attributes are in. hence had not made any progress on this. 
And i apologize for the delay in YARN-3409, will kick start that work shortly.
